description:
Two patterns surfaced by the sdf-hallucination pilot (stg-teu.4) that every memo user must know: (1) anything that affects a step's answer must travel through the step's INPUTS — module-level constants and closure values don't enter memo keys; bundle params into the mapped items. (2) Flow-level smoke=N truncates DAG fan-outs but in-step batch sizes (n samples per probe, panel sizes) need config cooperation — the standard pattern is a smoke-aware config feeding the cell dicts. Also note side-effect steps (figure writing) don't fit memoization (replay skips side effects) — keep them post-run as pure functions over results. Add to README memo section + a cookbook recipe.

## Notes
README side is DONE (config-first cheap-first section landed with PR #22); remaining scope = cookbook recipe + inputs-through-cells pattern write-up.
title: Document memoization usage patterns (inputs-through-cells, smoke config cooperation)
